content: Retrieval-augmented context management can be modified to store and prioritize *novel unexplored state-observation pairs* discovered by an ICM curiosity mechanism, turning the agent's external knowledge store into a curiosity-driven 'exploration memory' that avoids redundant novelty-seeking and directs inquiry toward under-explored high-information domains. This framework solves the common ICM limitation of wasting reward on repeatedly revisiting noisy, low-value novel observations.
